rates: Steady at 3.50-3.75% (held June 17). July hike probability ~17%, down from ~42% before the June CPI print.
inflation: June CPI -0.4% MoM vs -0.2% expected; +3.5% YoY vs 3.8% expected. Core CPI flat MoM vs +0.2% expected; +2.6% YoY vs 2.9% expected. Biggest monthly decline since April 2020.

PPI data today — another inflation reading (could cut either way)

note: CPI beat materially reduces near-term rate hike risk, but geopolitical tensions (US-Iran, Hormuz, oil at ~$85) remain live. Market valuation is slightly above historical averages (forward P/E ~20.5x vs 10yr avg ~19.0x). SPY is 8.2% above its 200-day average, meaning the market is not offering a broad discount. No watchlist names trade inside our margin-of-safety target. PPI and Warsh Senate testimony today add short-term event risk. Posture stays cautious.
